ogTitle: Kimbolton vs King Leopold Ranges: Climate Safety

heading: Kimbolton vs King Leopold Ranges — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kimbolton, Western Australia and King Leopold Ranges, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: King Leopold Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Kimbolton (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kimbolton or King Leopold Ranges safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kimbolton has a flood risk score of 49/100, while King Leopold Ranges scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kimbolton scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. King Leopold Ranges scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kimbolton or King Leopold Ranges?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. King Leopold Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
